body.title: Make the escalation ladder's rungs real — a model must declare which role-tier stack it can stand in
body.description:
BLOCKING PREREQUISITE for domain-owned + default escalation. VERIFIED 2026-07-08: ticket_tier=builder and ticket_tier=creator resolve to the SAME model (devstral-small-2:24b@ollama); ticket_tier=master resolves to None. Five role rungs (apprentice/builder/creator/master/guru) collapse into three difficulty buckets, and builder+creator both map to 'code'. And NO model is creator-tier: the model registry's tier vocabulary is (minion/worker/analyst/designer), which shares not one word with the role ladder. Two tier vocabularies that never meet. The old triples carried an explicit creator tier ('absorbs builder escalations before CC'); its only model was the phantom qwen/qwen3-30b-a3b-instruct@openrouter edge with no ModelSpec, dropped at the cutover. Creator tier has been dead; the dimensional rewrite made the deadness visible.

An escalation policy — the proxy's default OR a domain's own — has nothing to walk until asking for a 'creator-capable stack' can return a different model than a 'builder-capable stack'.

WORK: give a model a capability position on the ROLE ladder (or make 'stack' first-class: a named set of connections per rung), reconcile the two tier vocabularies, and ensure each rung selects a strictly more capable stack. Do not paper over by re-adding curated triples.

**Test plan:** red->green: resolve(ticket_tier=builder, domain=coding) and resolve(ticket_tier=creator, domain=coding) return DIFFERENT models, and each higher rung is strictly more capable; no rung resolves identically to the one below it.

**Decision:** decision:D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Made the escalation ladder's rungs real (MECHANISM fix, hermetically proven; conditions recorded per epistemology-experiment-only). Two-half fix: (1) injective role->measured-bucket projection (apprentice->classify, builder->code, creator->design, master->frontier) in dimensions._TIER_DIFFICULTY; guru is the human terminal (resolve() short-circuits to rules_engine.HUMAN_TERMINAL, above master). (2) least-over-provisioned selector tiebreak in resolve() (equal cost -> lowest bucket clearing the floor), so raising the rung strictly raises the selected bucket; cost still dominates (cloud fleet not re-stranded). Removed stale guru-work-is-design policy. MEASURED under LIVE default policies (policies=None, sources forced available to isolate the router from provider weather): GENERALIST domain ladder walks all four rungs DISTINCTLY (classify<code<design<frontier<terminal) — the real end-to-end win, also pinned by passing test_the_real_ladder_has_three_distinct_generalist_rungs. CODING domain tops out at 'code' under the coding-needs-tools policy: deepseek-r1:32b (design) and deepseek-v3.1:671b-cloud (frontier) declare no 'tools' feature, so creator/master resolve to None — a known registry/policy gap already pinned by passing test_the_coding_ladder_still_tops_out_and_that_is_not_hidden and ticketed as T-inference-activate-coding-tools-policy, NOT this ticket's scope. CORRECTION: the earlier 'coding: four distinct buckets' claim held only under policies=[] + forced-available (a mechanism illustration, not live behavior) — condition omitted in the first close, corrected here. Proof: tests/inference/test_tier_ladder_real.py::test_ladder_rungs_are_real_and_strictly_increasing (hermetic, red=AssertionError->green), cert @586bea81. Full inference suite 494 passed; 3 remaining fails pre-existing/environmental (capability_evidence x2, dicksimnel live smoke), confirmed on clean HEAD. Ordering traces only to measured difficulty_capable, no phantom rung. Follow-up: audit resolve() consumers for model-less HUMAN_TERMINAL decisions.
